wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the VaidyaSetu project overview section. Explain the platform's mission to bridge Allopathy, Ayurveda, and Homeopathy, its core purpose as an AI-powered digital health platform, and its position in the healthcare ecosystem. Document the project's architecture overview, key features including multi-disease risk assessment, drug interaction safety analysis, personalized recommendations, and emergency alert systems. Include the technology stack summary covering React frontend, Node.js backend, MongoDB Atlas database, and AI/ML integrations. Provide context about the implementation phases and current status. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and make content accessible to both healthcare professionals and technology stakeholders.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend architecture documentation for VaidyaSetu's Node.js/Express system. Document the layered architecture with MVC pattern implementation, explaining how routes handle HTTP requests, services encapsulate business logic, and models manage data access through Mongoose. Detail the modular routing system with 20+ API endpoints organized by functional domains (user management, health assessment, medication tracking, etc.). Explain middleware configuration for CORS, JSON parsing, and request processing. Document the service layer architecture including AI services, reminder services, and utility functions. Include database connection patterns, error handling strategies, and background job processing through cron jobs. Provide examples of route-to-service-to-model interactions and explain the separation of concerns across layers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for VaidyaSetu development setup. Document the complete development environment configuration including Node.js requirements, npm installation, and dependency setup for both backend and frontend. Explain the manual configuration steps required for Google Cloud APIs (Fitness API and Cloud Vision API), OAuth credentials setup, and .env file configuration. Provide step-by-step instructions for running the backend server with node server.js and frontend development server with npm run dev. Include troubleshooting common setup issues, port conflicts, and environment variable configuration. Document the project structure overview and initial project verification steps. Make content accessible to developers new to the codebase while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VaidyaSetu's user management system. Document the complete user lifecycle including authentication with Clerk, user profile creation and management, health data collection, preference settings, and data privacy controls. Explain the UserProfile model structure, user registration workflow, profile editing interface, and settings management. Include implementation details for user data validation, security measures, and compliance with healthcare data regulations. Document the integration between frontend components and backend APIs, including real-time updates and data synchronization. Address user onboarding flows, profile completeness tracking, and data governance features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation for VaidyaSetu's MongoDB implementation. Detail all entity relationships including UserProfile, Medication, Alert, Vital, Report, History, InteractionHistory, DrugMapping, Analytics, and ApiCache models. Document field definitions, data types, validation rules, and business constraints for each collection. Explain primary/foreign key relationships, indexing strategies, and query optimization patterns. Include sample data structures, schema evolution practices, and migration procedures. Document data access patterns, aggregation pipelines, and performance considerations. Address data integrity constraints, cascading operations, and referential consistency. Provide examples of complex queries and reporting scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VaidyaSetu's dashboard and navigation system. Document the main dashboard interface including layout structure, widget organization, and responsive design patterns. Detail the sidebar navigation component with menu items, routing integration, and mobile responsiveness. Explain the theme context system for dark/light mode switching and color scheme management. Include component props, state management, and integration with React Router for navigation. Provide examples of customizing navigation items, adding new dashboard widgets, and implementing theme-aware components. Address accessibility features, keyboard navigation, and screen reader compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for VaidyaSetu's authentication endpoints. Document user registration, login, logout, password reset, and session management APIs. Include Clerk authentication integration details, JWT token handling, and security headers. Specify request/response schemas for user creation, authentication verification, and profile updates. Document error codes for invalid credentials, account verification, and session expiration. Provide client-side implementation examples for React applications and mobile clients.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive routing system documentation for VaidyaSetu's Express.js API architecture. Document the modular routing pattern with 20+ API endpoints organized by functional domains (user management, health assessment, medication tracking, vitals monitoring, alerts, AI services, etc.). Explain route registration patterns, URL path conventions (/api/), and parameter handling. Detail request/response flow from routes to controllers, error handling middleware integration, and CORS configuration. Include examples of route definitions, parameter validation, and response formatting. Document the dual routing approach for interaction routes and explain how routes handle different HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE). Provide guidelines for adding new routes and maintaining consistency across the routing architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component architecture documentation for VaidyaSetu's React application. Document the hierarchical component structure starting from the root App.jsx down to specialized components like Chatbot, VitalsCharts, Sidebar, and disease cards. Explain component composition patterns, prop drilling solutions, and reusability principles. Detail the component lifecycle, state management within components, and inter-component communication patterns. Include the component folder structure, naming conventions, and styling approaches. Document reusable UI components, layout components, and specialized functional components. Address component testing strategies, performance optimization techniques, and accessibility considerations in the component architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for VaidyaSetu's layout components. Document the Sidebar component including navigation structure, menu items, and responsive behavior. Explain the App.jsx root component and its role in routing and global state management. Detail the ThemeContext implementation for theme switching and styling consistency. Include component props, state management, event handlers, and integration patterns. Provide examples of component usage, customization options, and accessibility features. Document the overall layout hierarchy and how components work together to create the application shell.

wiki_catalogs.name: Health Risk Assessment Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the VaidyaSetu health risk assessment engine. Detail the evidence-based algorithms used for calculating disease risk scores across 18+ conditions including diabetes, thyroid disorders, cardiovascular diseases, mental health conditions, and lifestyle-related illnesses. Explain the step-by-step risk calculation methodology including baseline prevalence data, demographic factors, lifestyle choices, family history, and protective factors. Document the Indian Diabetes Risk Score (IDRS) algorithm, likelihood ratio models for thyroid disorders, and disease-specific scoring mechanisms. Describe the mitigation library with personalized recommendations based on diet type, age, gender, and activity level. Explain the emergency alert system for critical symptoms and the doctor consultation trigger logic. Include examples of risk score calculations, scoring categories (Very Low to Very High), and clinical decision thresholds. Address the integration of Ayurvedic principles with modern epidemiological data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Risk Scoring Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the VaidyaSetu risk scoring algorithms. Explain the step-by-step methodology for calculating disease risk scores across 18+ conditions including diabetes, thyroid disorders, cardiovascular diseases, mental health conditions, and lifestyle-related illnesses. Document the evidence-based approach using baseline prevalence data, demographic factors, lifestyle choices, family history, and protective factors. Detail the Indian Diabetes Risk Score (IDRS) algorithm with age, waist circumference, physical activity, and family history components. Explain the likelihood ratio model for thyroid disorders using fatigue, weight changes, cold intolerance, and dry skin symptoms. Cover the generic scoring framework with factor addition/subtraction logic, protective factor application, and score normalization. Include examples of risk score calculations, scoring categories (Very Low to Very High), and clinical decision thresholds. Address the integration of Ayurvedic principles with modern epidemiological data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prescription Upload and OCR Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the prescription upload and OCR processing system. Explain the complete workflow from image upload to text extraction and medication data parsing. Document the supported image formats, size limitations, and quality requirements. Detail the OCR processing pipeline including image preprocessing, text recognition, and structured data extraction. Explain the integration with Tesseract.js for optical character recognition and the custom image processing utilities. Document the validation rules applied to extracted medication data including dosage units, frequency patterns, and drug name standardization. Include examples of successful and failed OCR processing scenarios, error handling strategies, and manual correction workflows. Address the handling of different prescription formats, handwritten vs printed text, and multilingual support.

wiki_catalogs.name: RAG Architecture and Vector Search
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architecture and vector search system. Explain the semantic search implementation using vector embeddings stored in MongoDB Atlas vector search. Document the knowledge base organization across multiple medical domains including Ayurveda, Homeopathy, allopathic research, and WHO guidelines. Detail the embedding generation process, vector indexing strategy, and similarity search algorithms. Explain how the system retrieves relevant medical literature and guidelines based on user symptoms, conditions, and treatment queries. Include examples of vector search queries, similarity thresholds, and result ranking mechanisms. Address the integration with MongoDB Atlas Vector Search and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for VaidyaSetu development setup. Include step-by-step installation instructions for both backend and frontend environments, database configuration with MongoDB Atlas, API key setup for external services (Google Cloud Vision, Groq), and environment variable configuration. Document prerequisite knowledge requirements including JavaScript/Node.js, React, RESTful APIs, and MongoDB basics. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues, verification steps to ensure proper installation, and guidance for first-time contributors. Include development workflow, hot reload setup, and debugging techniques. Make content beginner-friendly while ensuring experienced developers can quickly get up and running.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend architecture documentation for VaidyaSetu's React 19 application. Document the component-based architecture with reusable UI components, page-level components, and context providers for state management. Explain the routing system, navigation patterns, and responsive design implementation. Detail the component hierarchy from root App.jsx down to specialized components like Chatbot, VitalsCharts, and disease cards. Document the state management approach using React hooks and custom context providers. Include the theming system, asset management, and progressive web app capabilities. Explain component composition patterns, prop drilling solutions, and performance optimization techniques. Address mobile-first design principles and accessibility compliance throughout the frontend architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for VaidyaSetu system design. Describe the high-level architecture showing the React frontend, Node.js/Express backend, and MongoDB Atlas database integration. Document the layered architecture pattern with presentation layer (React components), business logic layer (Express routes and services), and data access layer (Mongoose models). Explain the microservice-style API design with modular route handlers for different functional domains. Detail the AI/ML integration architecture including Groq SDK usage, vector search capabilities, and knowledge base implementation. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Document the authentication architecture using Clerk, state management patterns, and real-time communication mechanisms. Address scalability considerations, security patterns, and deployment topology.

wiki_catalogs.name: Health Risk Assessment Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for VaidyaSetu's sophisticated health risk assessment engine. Document the evidence-based algorithms used for calculating disease risk scores, including the Indian Diabetes Risk Score (IDRS), likelihood ratio models, and demographic risk factors. Explain the step-by-step calculation process for each disease, protective factors, and emergency detection mechanisms. Detail the risk scoring categories, mitigation library integration, and specialist mapping system. Include implementation examples showing how user profiles are processed, risk factors are evaluated, and personalized recommendations are generated. Document the emergency alert system, consultation triggers, and cultural intelligence in risk assessment. Address performance optimization, data quality requirements, and validation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Knowledge Base Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed knowledge base management documentation for VaidyaSetu's AI-powered medical knowledge system. Document the vector search architecture using MongoDB Atlas Vector Search, including embedding generation, chunking strategies, and semantic similarity algorithms. Explain the multi-source medical literature integration from AYUSH, CCRH, DrugBank, ICMR, IMPPAT, PubMed, and WHO databases. Detail the chunking and preprocessing pipeline, embedding generation workflows, and vector storage optimization. Document the master index management, chunk metadata organization, and retrieval mechanisms. Include configuration guides for Atlas Vector Search setup, embedding model selection, and performance tuning. Provide examples of knowledge retrieval queries, similarity scoring, and relevance ranking. Address data freshness, update strategies, and quality assurance processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Health Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for health profile management components. Document the HealthProfile page showing user health data visualization, progress tracking, and summary statistics. Detail the ProfileEditor component for editing personal information, health metrics, and preferences. Explain the ChangeHistory interface for viewing activity logs and data modifications. Cover the onboarding flow components including biometric input, lifestyle questions, diet assessment, women's health, respiratory health, mental health, and medical history collection. Include form validation patterns, data persistence, user experience flows, and integration with backend health data APIs. Address progressive disclosure techniques, accessibility compliance, and responsive form layouts.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for user profile management endpoints. Document GET/PUT operations for retrieving and updating user health profiles, personal information, and preferences. Include health data collection endpoints for biometrics, lifestyle factors, and medical history. Specify validation rules for age, weight, height, and other health metrics. Document profile completion status tracking and onboarding progress APIs. Include data privacy controls and consent management endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed service layer documentation for VaidyaSetu's business logic architecture. Document the service abstraction pattern that encapsulates complex operations like AI recommendations, reminder generation, alert processing, and OCR functionality. Explain service initialization, dependency injection patterns, and inter-service communication. Detail the AI service integration with Groq SDK, reminder service background processing, and vision services for image analysis. Include examples of service method signatures, parameter validation, and error handling strategies. Document service lifecycle management, memory optimization, and concurrent request handling. Provide guidelines for creating new services and maintaining service boundaries for clean architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed routing and navigation documentation for VaidyaSetu's React Router implementation. Document the protected route system using Clerk authentication, route guards, and navigation patterns. Explain the sidebar navigation structure, breadcrumb implementation, and responsive navigation behavior. Detail the route configuration, nested routing patterns, and dynamic route handling. Include navigation state management, active link highlighting, and smooth scrolling behavior. Document the authentication flow integration with routing, user session validation, and automatic redirection patterns. Address mobile navigation considerations and accessibility in navigation components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Health Dashboard Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for VaidyaSetu's health dashboard components. Document the SavedDoctorsWidget for managing healthcare providers, VitalsCharts for health metric visualization, and VitalsModals for input forms. Explain component data binding, chart rendering with Three.js, modal dialog implementations, and state synchronization. Include examples of component integration, data flow patterns, and user interaction handling. Document styling approaches, responsive design considerations, and accessibility features specific to health data visualization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Drug Interaction Safety System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the VaidyaSetu drug interaction safety system. Explain the real-time interaction analysis engine that evaluates potential adverse reactions between prescribed medications and herbal supplements. Document the interaction database integration with RxNorm API for standardized drug terminology and the comprehensive interaction dataset covering 1000+ drug-drug and drug-herb combinations. Detail the severity classification system (mild, moderate, severe, contraindicated) and clinical significance ratings. Explain the automated safety checking process during prescription entry and medication schedule updates. Document the alternative treatment recommendations and therapeutic substitution suggestions generated by the system. Include examples of common interaction scenarios, safety thresholds, and clinical decision support workflows. Address the integration with Ayurvedic herbs and homeopathic preparations alongside conventional pharmaceuticals.

wiki_catalogs.name: Disease-Specific Calculators
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for disease-specific risk calculation modules in VaidyaSetu. Detail the specialized algorithms for each disease category including diabetes (IDRS scoring), thyroid disorders (likelihood ratio model), anemia (dietary and supplementation factors), asthma and COPD (symptom and environmental triggers), mental health conditions (depression and anxiety screening), PCOS (gender-specific factors), and general risk factors (BMI, smoking, activity). Explain the conditional logic for each calculator, input requirements, scoring weights, and interpretation guidelines. Document the missing data handling for critical symptoms and the emergency symptom capture system. Include practical examples showing how different user profiles affect disease-specific risk scores, typical score ranges for each condition, and the rationale behind disease-specific scoring weights.

wiki_catalogs.name: Medication Tracking and Scheduling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the medication tracking and scheduling system. Explain the medication data model including dosage schedules, frequency patterns, and timing preferences. Document the CRUD operations for medication management with proper validation and error handling. Detail the adherence tracking mechanism with dose history, missed doses, and compliance metrics. Explain the integration with risk scoring algorithms to ensure safe medication combinations and timing. Document the medication history tracking with refill reminders, expiration monitoring, and treatment duration management. Include examples of medication entry workflows, schedule modifications, and adherence reporting dashboards. Address the handling of complex medication regimens, split dosing schedules, and special instructions. Explain the relationship between medications and health risk assessments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Source Knowledge Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the multi-source knowledge integration system. Detail how VaidyaSetu aggregates and processes medical knowledge from diverse sources including AYUSH publications, CCRH homeopathy guidelines, PubMed research articles, WHO recommendations, and IMPPAT interaction data. Explain the data ingestion pipeline, text preprocessing workflows, and knowledge normalization processes. Document the master index structure that coordinates different knowledge domains and enables cross-domain reasoning. Include examples of knowledge fusion techniques, conflict resolution strategies between traditional and modern medicine sources, and quality assurance measures for medical information validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for VaidyaSetu's system design. Describe the high-level layered architecture with presentation layer (React frontend), business logic layer (Express.js routes and services), data access layer (Mongoose models), and integration layer (external APIs). Document component interactions, data flows between frontend and backend, API communication patterns, and microservice architecture principles. Explain technical decisions around the MVC pattern, repository pattern, and service layer approach. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams showing user interactions, data persistence, and external service integrations. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, caching, error handling, and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database design documentation for VaidyaSetu's MongoDB Atlas implementation. Document the schema design for core entities including UserProfile, Medication, Alert, Vital, and Report models. Explain the relationships between collections, embedding vs referencing strategies, and indexing patterns for optimal query performance. Detail the data validation rules, required fields, and optional properties for each model. Document the Mongoose schema definitions, virtual properties, and middleware functions. Include data lifecycle management, audit trails, and data retention policies. Explain the aggregation pipelines used for complex queries and reporting. Address data security considerations, access control patterns, and backup strategies for healthcare data compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for VaidyaSetu core features and capabilities. Document the consultation trigger system, alternative medication suggestions, and healthcare provider notification workflows. Address data quality validation, transformation rules, and consistency checks throughout the pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mitigation Recommendations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the VaidyaSetu mitigation recommendation system. Explain the cultural intelligence embedded in recommendations including Ayurvedic dietary principles, traditional exercise practices, and indigenous health wisdom. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the intelligent reminder and notification system. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI prompt engineering system that creates context-aware recommendations. Detail the context injection strategies that merge patient data with retrieved medical knowledge. Explain the design token system, color palette management, and typography hierarchy. Detail the responsive design approach, breakpoint management, and mobile-first principles. Address accessibility considerations in theming, contrast ratios, and inclusive design practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Onboarding Components

Document styling consistency, accessibility features, and mobile-responsive design patterns used throughout the onboarding process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Medication Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the VaidyaSetu medication management system. Explain the comprehensive prescription handling workflow including OCR-based prescription parsing, medication database matching, and automated medication entry. Document the medication tracking system with dosage schedules, frequency monitoring, and adherence visualization. Detail the reminder service with intelligent alert generation based on medication timing, food interactions, and patient preferences. Explain the prescription upload process with image preprocessing, text extraction, and data validation. Document the medication history tracking, refill reminders, and interaction conflict detection. Include examples of medication entry workflows, reminder scheduling patterns, and adherence reporting. Address the integration of Ayurvedic formulations, homeopathic remedies, and conventional medications within a unified management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Emergency Alert System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the VaidyaSetu emergency alert detection and response system. Detail the critical symptom capture mechanism that identifies life-threatening conditions including chest pain, severe shortness of breath, severe headaches, vision changes, suicidal thoughts, abdominal pain, and severe vomiting. Explain the Step 58 emergency scoring logic that flags critical symptoms requiring immediate medical attention. Document the emergency alert generation process, severity categorization, and automatic escalation protocols. Detail the integration with doctor consultation triggers and the decision matrix for urgent vs. routine care. Include examples of emergency scenarios, typical emergency alert responses, and the clinical decision thresholds that trigger emergency protocols. Address the safety considerations, red flag identification, and the importance of immediate medical intervention.

wiki_catalogs.name: Drug Interaction Safety Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the drug interaction safety analysis system. Explain the interaction detection algorithms that analyze concurrent medications for potential adverse reactions. Document the integration with RxNav API for real-time drug information and interaction checking. Detail the interaction severity scoring system and warning classification levels. Explain the Ayurvedic and homeopathic interaction database integration alongside conventional pharmaceutical interactions. Document the conflict resolution recommendations including alternative medications, timing adjustments, and monitoring requirements. Include examples of interaction scenarios, safety thresholds, and clinical intervention triggers. Address the handling of polypharmacy cases, herb-drug interactions, and laboratory interaction monitoring. Explain the integration with emergency alert systems for critical interaction warnings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Recommendation Generation and Personalization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the recommendation generation system that produces personalized health insights. Document the recommendation categorization into dietary, lifestyle, and monitoring domains with priority assignment. Include examples of generated recommendations, confidence scoring mechanisms, and cultural adaptation strategies. Address the integration with traditional medicine wisdom and evidence-based clinical guidelines, including conflict detection and resolution between different therapeutic approaches.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# VaidyaSetu: Comprehensive Digital Health Platform

## Project Introduction

VaidyaSetu is an innovative digital health platform designed to revolutionize healthcare accessibility and management in India through intelligent technology integration. The platform serves as a comprehensive health ecosystem that combines AI-powered risk assessment, real-time drug interaction analysis, personalized health recommendations, and seamless patient-doctor connectivity.

### Purpose Statement
The platform aims to bridge the gap between traditional Ayurvedic practices and modern digital health solutions, providing evidence-based health insights while respecting indigenous medical wisdom. It focuses on preventive care, early intervention, and personalized health management tailored to the Indian population's unique health needs.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Early Detection**: Implement AI-driven risk scoring for major non-communicable diseases (NCDs)
- **Drug Safety**: Provide real-time interaction warnings and contraindication alerts
- **Personalized Care**: Deliver culturally appropriate health recommendations
- **Accessibility**: Make quality healthcare information available to underserved populations
- **Integration**: Seamlessly connect patients with qualified healthcare providers

### Target Audience
- Health-conscious individuals seeking preventive care
- Patients managing chronic conditions
- Healthcare providers looking for decision-support tools
- Ayurvedic practitioners integrating modern technology
- Government health initiatives targeting NCD prevention

## Technical Architecture

VaidyaSetu follows a modern microservices architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, and data processing layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend React App] --> B[Express.js API Server]
    B --> C[MongoDB Atlas]
    B --> D[External APIs]
    D --> E[RxNav API]
    D --> F[OpenFDA API]
    D --> G[Groq AI]
    D --> H[Google Vision AI]
    B --> I[Vector Store]
    I --> J[Knowledge Chunks]
    B --> K[OCR Services]
    K --> L[Tesseract Engine]
    B --> M[Caching Layer]
    M --> N[Redis Cache]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several interconnected layers:

**Presentation Layer**: React-based frontend with modern UI/UX design, supporting both web and progressive web app functionality.

**Application Layer**: Node.js/Express backend handling business logic, API orchestration, and service coordination.

**Data Layer**: MongoDB Atlas for structured health data storage with vector search capabilities for knowledge retrieval.

**Integration Layer**: External API integrations for drug interaction data, medical literature, and AI services.

**Processing Layer**: Vector search engine for semantic knowledge retrieval and OCR processing for prescription analysis.

### Design Patterns

The platform implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: For database abstraction and data access
- **Service Layer Pattern**: For business logic encapsulation
- **Factory Pattern**: For creating different types of health recommendations
- **Observer Pattern**: For real-time alert systems and notifications
- **Strategy Pattern**: For different risk calculation methodologies

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Interface
    participant S as Server
    participant DB as Database
    participant AI as AI Services
    participant EX as External APIs
    
    U->>S: Health Assessment Request
    S->>DB: Retrieve User Profile
    S->>AI: Generate Risk Analysis
    AI->>EX: Fetch Drug Interactions
    EX-->>AI: Interaction Data
    AI-->>S: Risk Assessment Results
    S->>DB: Store Assessment History
    S-->>U: Comprehensive Health Report
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Server**: `backend/server.js` serves as the central application entry point, initializing Express.js, connecting to MongoDB, and configuring all route handlers and middleware.

**Frontend Application**: `frontend/src/App.jsx` manages the React application lifecycle, authentication flow, and routing between different health modules.

### Core Modules

**Risk Assessment Engine**: `backend/src/utils/riskScorer.js` implements sophisticated algorithms for calculating disease risk scores based on Indian epidemiological data and personalized health profiles.

**AI-Powered Recommendations**: `backend/src/services/aiService.js` leverages Groq's LLM capabilities for generating personalized health mitigation strategies.

**Knowledge Retrieval**: `backend/src/utils/ragRetriever.js` implements a Retrieval-Augmented Generation system for semantic search across medical literature and drug databases.

**Interaction Analysis**: `backend/src/utils/interactionEngine.js` processes complex drug interaction scenarios using fuzzy matching and comprehensive interaction databases.

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**: Both frontend and backend utilize `.env` files for environment-specific settings including API keys, database connections, and feature flags.

**Database Schema**: `backend/src/models/` directory contains Mongoose schemas defining the data structures for user profiles, medications, alerts, and health metrics.

**API Documentation**: `API_DOCS.md` provides comprehensive documentation for all REST endpoints and integration points.

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies** (`backend/package.json`):
- Express.js for web server functionality
- Mongoose for MongoDB object modeling
- Groq SDK for AI-powered recommendations
- Tesseract.js for OCR processing
- Fuse.js for fuzzy search capabilities

**Frontend Dependencies** (`frontend/package.json`):
- React 19 for component-based UI development
- React Router for navigation
- Three.js for 3D body visualization
- Framer Motion for smooth animations
- Zustand for state management

### Integration Points

**Medical Data Sources**: Integration with RxNav for drug interaction data, OpenFDA for adverse event reporting, and various Ayurvedic/Homoeopathic databases.

**Authentication**: Clerk integration for secure user authentication and session management.

**Analytics**: Real-time health tracking and trend analysis capabilities.

**Notification System**: Multi-channel alert delivery for medication reminders and health updates.

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class UserProfile {
        +clerkId: String
        +name: FieldSchema
        +age: FieldSchema
        +settings: Object
        +onboardingComplete: Boolean
    }
    
    class Medication {
        +name: String
        +dosage: String
        +frequency: String
        +timings: Array
        +active: Boolean
    }
    
    class Alert {
        +type: String
        +priority: String
        +title: String
        +status: String
    }
    
    class RiskAssessment {
        +calculateDetailedInsights()
        +selectMitigations()
        +getScoreCategory()
    }
    
    UserProfile --> Medication : manages
    UserProfile --> Alert : generates
    RiskAssessment --> UserProfile : analyzes
    RiskAssessment --> Medication : evaluates
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Comprehensive Health Assessment**: Multi-disease risk scoring system incorporating Indian epidemiological data and personalized health factors.

**Real-Time Drug Interaction Analysis**: Advanced algorithm for detecting potentially dangerous drug combinations with detailed severity ratings.

**Personalized Health Recommendations**: Culturally appropriate dietary and lifestyle suggestions based on regional ingredients and traditional practices.

**Emergency Alert System**: Critical symptom detection with immediate guidance and emergency contact protocols.

**Medication Management**: Smart scheduling, adherence tracking, and refill reminders with integration to pharmacy services.

### Implementation Highlights

**AI-Powered Insights**: `backend/src/utils/ragRetriever.js` enables semantic search across millions of medical documents for evidence-based recommendations.

**Cultural Intelligence**: `backend/src/utils/mitigationLibrary.js` contains regionally-appropriate health recommendations aligned with Indian dietary and lifestyle patterns.

**Real-Time Monitoring**: `backend/src/services/reminderService.js` coordinates automated health alerts and follow-up notifications.

**Visual Health Tracking**: `frontend/src/components/VitalsCharts.jsx` provides interactive charts for monitoring health metrics over time.

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> UserRegistration
    UserRegistration --> HealthProfileSetup
    HealthProfileSetup --> RiskAssessment
    RiskAssessment --> ResultReview
    ResultReview --> RecommendationGeneration
    RecommendationGeneration --> MedicationManagement
    MedicationManagement --> Monitoring
    Monitoring --> RiskReassessment
    RiskReassessment --> Monitoring
    Monitoring --> EmergencyAlert
    EmergencyAlert --> MedicalConsultation
    MedicalConsultation --> [*]
```

The platform's modular architecture ensures scalability, maintainability, and adaptability to future healthcare innovations while maintaining focus on its core mission of making quality healthcare accessible to all Indians.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](backend/package.json)
- [server.js](backend/server.js)
- [App.jsx](frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [UserProfile.js](backend/src/models/UserProfile.js)
- [ragRetriever.js](backend/src/utils/ragRetriever.js)
- [aiService.js](backend/src/services/aiService.js)
- [riskScorer.js](backend/src/utils/riskScorer.js)
- [interactionEngine.js](backend/src/utils/interactionEngine.js)
- [Medication.js](backend/src/models/Medication.js)
- [Alert.js](backend/src/models/Alert.js)
- [mitigationLibrary.js](backend/src/utils/mitigationLibrary.js)
- [emergencyScorer.js](backend/src/utils/emergencyScorer.js)
- [prevalenceData.js](backend/src/utils/prevalenceData.js)
- [specialistMapping.js](backend/src/utils/specialistMapping.js)

</blog>
